1→package agent

    21→const systemPrompt = `You are a media curation assistant for a home media server. You help users manage their TV series library through Sonarr, their movie library through Radarr, and handle media requests through Overseerr.
    22→
    23→Your capabilities:
    24→- Search for TV series and movies, and provide concise summaries of results
    25→- Add series or movies to the library for monitoring and automatic downloading
    26→- Check the download queue for active and pending downloads (both TV and movies)
    27→- Review download history for recent activity
    28→- Monitor system health and report any issues
    29→- Remove failed downloads and manage the blocklist
    30→- Get detailed series info and episode status
    31→- Update season monitoring settings (enable/disable monitoring per season)
    32→- Search for available releases and see why they were accepted/rejected
    33→- View Sonarr logs for debugging
    34→- Check quality profiles, blocklist, root folders, and download client status
    35→- Manage indexers via Prowlarr: list, test, enable/disable, update priority, delete, check stats and health, search across indexers
    36→- List, approve, decline, delete, and retry media requests from Overseerr
    37→- Get detailed information about specific requests
    38→- Search for movies and TV shows in Overseerr's media database
    39→- Get request statistics (pending, approved, declined counts)
    40→
    41→Guidelines:
    42→- When searching, present results concisely with title, year, and a brief description
    43→- Always confirm with the user before adding a new series or movie, or approving/declining requests
    44→- When reporting health issues, clearly explain what each issue means and suggest fixes
    45→- Be direct and helpful — avoid unnecessary pleasantries
    46→- Only use the tools provided — do not make up information
    47→- You have a persistent notebook for memory across conversations. Save useful observations: user preferences, recurring issues, operational patterns
    48→- Pinned notes are always visible to you; reference notes need to be looked up with notebook_read
    49→- Keep pinned notes concise and high-signal; use reference type for detailed information
    50→- Before creating a new note, check existing notes to avoid duplicates — update instead if a similar note exists`
    51→
    52→const maxToolRounds = 10
    53→
    54→// Agent handles natural language interactions using Claude with Sonarr, Radarr, Prowlarr, and Overseerr tools.
    55→type Agent struct {
    56→	client    *anthropic.Client
    57→	sonarr    sonarr.Client
    58→	radarr    radarr.Client
    59→	prowlarr  prowlarr.Client
    60→	overseerr overseerr.Client
    61→	notebook  notebook.Notebook
    62→	weather   *weather.Client
    63→	model     string
    64→	maxTok    int64
    65→	logger    *slog.Logger
    66→	limiter   *RateLimiter
    67→}
    68→
    69→func New(apiKey string, sonarrClient sonarr.Client, radarrClient radarr.Client, prowlarrClient prowlarr.Client, overseerrClient overseerr.Client, nb notebook.Notebook, weatherClient *weather.Client, model string, maxTokens int, logger *slog.Logger, limiter *RateLimiter) *Agent {
    70→	client := anthropic.NewClient(option.WithAPIKey(apiKey))
    71→	return &Agent{
    72→		client:    &client,
    73→		sonarr:    sonarrClient,
    74→		radarr:    radarrClient,
    75→		prowlarr:  prowlarrClient,
    76→		overseerr: overseerrClient,
    77→		notebook:  nb,
    78→		weather:   weatherClient,
    79→		model:     model,
    80→		maxTok:    int64(maxTokens),
    81→		logger:    logger,
    82→		limiter:   limiter,
    83→	}
    84→}
    85→
    86→// NewWithClient creates an Agent with a pre-configured Anthropic client (for testing).
    87→func NewWithClient(client *anthropic.Client, sonarrClient sonarr.Client, radarrClient radarr.Client, prowlarrClient prowlarr.Client, overseerrClient overseerr.Client, nb notebook.Notebook, weatherClient *weather.Client, model string, maxTokens int, logger *slog.Logger, limiter *RateLimiter) *Agent {
    88→	return &Agent{
    89→		client:    client,
    90→		sonarr:    sonarrClient,
    91→		radarr:    radarrClient,
    92→		prowlarr:  prowlarrClient,
    93→		overseerr: overseerrClient,
    94→		notebook:  nb,
    95→		weather:   weatherClient,
    96→		model:     model,
    97→		maxTok:    int64(maxTokens),
    98→		logger:    logger,
    99→		limiter:   limiter,
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// requestIDKey is the context key for the per-request identifier used in audit logs.
   104→type requestIDKey struct{}
   105→
   106→// WithRequestID returns a child context carrying the given request ID.
   107→func WithRequestID(ctx context.Context, id string) context.Context {
   108→	return context.WithValue(ctx, requestIDKey{}, id)
   109→}
   110→
   111→func requestID(ctx context.Context) string {
   112→	if v, ok := ctx.Value(requestIDKey{}).(string); ok {
   113→		return v
   114→	}
   115→	return ""
   116→}
   117→
   118→// buildSystemPrompt returns the system prompt with pinned notebook notes appended.
   119→func (a *Agent) buildSystemPrompt(ctx context.Context) string {
   120→	dateLine := fmt.Sprintf("Today's date is %s.", time.Now().Format("2006-01-02"))
   121→
   122→	var locationLine string
   123→	if a.weather != nil {
   124→		if cond := a.weather.Current(ctx); cond != nil {
   125→			locationLine = fmt.Sprintf("Current location: %s. Weather: %.0f°C, %s.", a.weather.Location(), cond.Temperature, cond.Description)
   126→		} else {
   127→			locationLine = fmt.Sprintf("Current location: %s.", a.weather.Location())
   128→		}
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	var prompt string
   132→	if locationLine != "" {
   133→		prompt = fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s\n\n%s", dateLine, locationLine, systemPrompt)
   134→	} else {
   135→		prompt = fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n%s", dateLine, systemPrompt)
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	if a.notebook == nil {
   139→		return prompt
   140→	}
   141→
   142→	pinned, err := a.notebook.Pinned(ctx)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		a.logger.Warn("failed to load pinned notes", "error", err)
   145→		return prompt
   146→	}
   147→	if len(pinned) == 0 {
   148→		return prompt
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	var sb strings.Builder
   152→	sb.WriteString(prompt)
   153→	sb.WriteString("\n\n## Notebook (always loaded)\n")
   154→	for _, n := range pinned {
   155→		sb.WriteString("### ")
   156→		sb.WriteString(n.Title)
   157→		sb.WriteString("\n")
   158→		sb.WriteString(n.Content)
   159→		sb.WriteString("\n")
   160→	}
   161→	return sb.String()
   162→}
   163→
   164→// Process runs the agentic tool-use loop for a user message and returns the final text response.
   165→// History turns are prepended to provide conversational context.
   166→func (a *Agent) Process(ctx context.Context, userMessage string, history []Turn) (string, error) {
   167→	tools := toolDefinitions()
   168→
   169→	var messages []anthropic.MessageParam
   170→	for _, t := range history {
   171→		switch t.Role {
   172→		case "user":
   173→			messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(t.Content)))
   174→		case "assistant":
   175→			messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewAssistantMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(t.Content)))
   176→		}
   177→	}
   178→	messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(userMessage)))
   179→
   180→	if a.limiter != nil {
   181→		a.limiter.Reset()
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	reqID := requestID(ctx)
   185→	sysPrompt := a.buildSystemPrompt(ctx)
   186→
   187→	for round := range maxToolRounds {
   188→		resp, err := a.client.Messages.New(ctx, anthropic.MessageNewParams{
   189→			Model:     anthropic.Model(a.model),
   190→			MaxTokens: a.maxTok,
   191→			System: []anthropic.TextBlockParam{
   192→				{Text: sysPrompt},
   193→			},
   194→			Messages: messages,
   195→			Tools:    tools,
   196→		})
   197→		if err != nil {
   198→			return "", fmt.Errorf("claude API call: %w", err)
   199→		}
   200→
   201→		// Collect tool uses from the response
   202→		var toolResults []anthropic.ContentBlockParamUnion
   203→		var textResponse string
   204→
   205→		for _, block := range resp.Content {
   206→			switch v := block.AsAny().(type) {
   207→			case anthropic.TextBlock:
   208→				textResponse += v.Text
   209→			case anthropic.ToolUseBlock:
   210→				result, isErr := a.executeToolWithAudit(ctx, v.Name, v.Input, round, reqID)
   211→				toolResults = append(toolResults, anthropic.NewToolResultBlock(v.ID, result, isErr))
   212→			}
   213→		}
   214→
   215→		// If no tool calls, we're done
   216→		if len(toolResults) == 0 {
   217→			return textResponse, nil
   218→		}
   219→
   220→		// Add assistant response and tool results to conversation
   221→		messages = append(messages, resp.ToParam())
   222→		messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(toolResults...))
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	return "", fmt.Errorf("exceeded maximum tool rounds (%d)", maxToolRounds)
   226→}
   227→
   228→// executeToolWithAudit wraps tool dispatch with rate limiting and audit logging.
   229→func (a *Agent) executeToolWithAudit(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage, round int, reqID string) (string, bool) {
   230→	// Audit log: invocation
   231→	a.logger.Info("tool invocation",
   232→		"tool", name,
   233→		"input", sanitizeInput(rawInput),
   234→		"round", round,
   235→		"request_id", reqID,
   236→	)
   237→
   238→	// Rate limit check
   239→	if a.limiter != nil {
   240→		if err := a.limiter.Allow(name, IsDestructive(name)); err != nil {
   241→			a.logger.Warn("tool rate limited",
   242→				"tool", name,
   243→				"error", err,
   244→				"round", round,
   245→				"request_id", reqID,
   246→			)
   247→			return jsonError(err.Error()), true
   248→		}
   249→	}
   250→
   251→	start := time.Now()
   252→	result, isErr := a.dispatchTool(ctx, name, rawInput)
   253→	duration := time.Since(start)
   254→
   255→	// Audit log: result
   256→	a.logger.Info("tool result",
   257→		"tool", name,
   258→		"success", !isErr,
   259→		"duration_ms", duration.Milliseconds(),
   260→		"round", round,
   261→		"request_id", reqID,
   262→	)
   263→
   264→	return result, isErr
   265→}
   266→
   267→// sanitizeInput returns a string summary of tool input suitable for logging.
   268→// It strips any field whose key contains sensitive substrings to avoid leaking credentials.
   269→func sanitizeInput(raw json.RawMessage) string {
   270→	var m map[string]any
   271→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
   272→		return "<invalid json>"
   273→	}
   274→	for k := range m {
   275→		lowerK := strings.ToLower(k)
   276→		if strings.Contains(lowerK, "key") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "secret") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "password") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "token") {
   277→			m[k] = "REDACTED"
   278→		}
   279→	}
   280→	out, err := json.Marshal(m)
   281→	if err != nil {
   282→		return "<failed to sanitize json>"
   283→	}
   284→	return string(out)
   285→}
   286→
   287→// dispatchTool executes a tool call and returns the JSON result string and whether it's an error.
   288→func (a *Agent) dispatchTool(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage) (string, bool) {
   289→	var result any
   290→	var err error
   291→
   292→	switch name {
   293→	case "search_series":
   294→		var input searchSeriesInput
   295→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   296→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   297→		}
   298→		result, err = a.sonarr.Search(ctx, input.Term)
   299→
   300→	case "add_series":
   301→		var input addSeriesInput
   302→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   303→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   304→		}
   305→		result, err = a.sonarr.Add(ctx, sonarr.AddSeriesRequest{
   306→			Title:            input.Title,
   307→			TVDBID:           input.TVDBID,
   308→			QualityProfileID: input.QualityProfileID,
   309→			RootFolderPath:   input.RootFolderPath,
   310→			Monitored:        true,
   311→			SeasonFolder:     true,
   312→		})
   313→
   314→	case "get_queue":
   315→		result, err = a.sonarr.Queue(ctx)
   316→
   317→	case "get_history":
   318→		var input getHistoryInput
   319→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   320→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   321→		}
   322→		pageSize := input.PageSize
   323→		if pageSize == 0 {
   324→			pageSize = 20
   325→		}
   326→		result, err = a.sonarr.History(ctx, pageSize)
   327→
   328→	case "check_health":
   329→		result, err = a.sonarr.Health(ctx)
   330→
   331→	case "remove_failed":
   332→		var input removeFailedInput
   333→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   334→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   335→		}
   336→		err = a.sonarr.RemoveFailed(ctx, input.ID, input.Blocklist)
   337→		if err == nil {
   338→			result = map[string]string{"status": "removed"}
   339→		}
   340→
   341→	case "get_series_detail":
   342→		var input getSeriesDetailInput
   343→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   344→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   345→		}
   346→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetSeries(ctx, input.SeriesID)
   347→
   348→	case "get_episodes":
   349→		var input getEpisodesInput
   350→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   351→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   352→		}
   353→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetEpisodes(ctx, input.SeriesID)
   354→
   355→	case "get_logs":
   356→		var input getLogsInput
   357→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   358→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   359→		}
   360→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetLogs(ctx, input.PageSize, input.Level)
   361→
   362→	case "manual_search":
   363→		var input manualSearchInput
   364→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   365→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   366→		}
   367→		result, err = a.sonarr.ManualSearch(ctx, input.EpisodeID)
   368→
   369→	case "get_quality_profiles":
   370→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetQualityProfiles(ctx)
   371→
   372→	case "get_blocklist":
   373→		var input getBlocklistInput
   374→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   375→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   376→		}
   377→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetBlocklist(ctx, input.PageSize)
   378→
   379→	case "get_root_folders":
   380→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetRootFolders(ctx)
   381→
   382→	case "get_download_clients":
   383→		result, err = a.sonarr.GetDownloadClients(ctx)
   384→
   385→	case "update_series_monitoring":
   386→		var input updateSeriesMonitoringInput
   387→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   388→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   389→		}
   390→		series, getErr := a.sonarr.GetSeries(ctx, input.SeriesID)
   391→		if getErr != nil {
   392→			return jsonError(getErr.Error()), true
   393→		}
   394→		found := false
   395→		for i, s := range series.Seasons {
   396→			if s.SeasonNumber == input.SeasonNumber {
   397→				series.Seasons[i].Monitored = input.Monitored
   398→				found = true
   399→				break
   400→			}
   401→		}
   402→		if !found {
   403→			return jsonError(fmt.Sprintf("season %d not found in series %q", input.SeasonNumber, series.Title)), true
   404→		}
   405→		result, err = a.sonarr.UpdateSeries(ctx, series)
   406→
   407→	case "trigger_series_search":
   408→		var input triggerSeriesSearchInput
   409→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   410→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   411→		}
   412→		cmd := sonarr.CommandRequest{
   413→			Name:     "SeriesSearch",
   414→			SeriesID: input.SeriesID,
   415→		}
   416→		if input.SeasonNumber != nil {
   417→			cmd.Name = "SeasonSearch"
   418→			cmd.SeasonNumber = input.SeasonNumber
   419→		}
   420→		result, err = a.sonarr.Command(ctx, cmd)
   421→
   422→	case "delete_series":
   423→		var input deleteSeriesInput
   424→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   425→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   426→		}
   427→		err = a.sonarr.DeleteSeries(ctx, input.SeriesID, input.DeleteFiles)
   428→		if err == nil {
   429→			result = map[string]string{"status": "deleted"}
   430→		}
   431→
   432→	case "remove_blocklist_item":
   433→		var input removeBlocklistItemInput
   434→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   435→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   436→		}
   437→		err = a.sonarr.DeleteBlocklistItem(ctx, input.ID)
   438→		if err == nil {
   439→			result = map[string]string{"status": "removed"}
   440→		}
   441→
   442→	case "grab_release":
   443→		var input grabReleaseInput
   444→		if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   445→			return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   446→		}
   447→		result, err = a.sonarr.GrabRelease(ctx, input.GUID, input.IndexerID)
   448→
   449→	case "search_movies", "add_movie", "get_movie_queue", "get_movie_history", "check_movie_health", "remove_failed_movie",
   450→		"get_movie_detail", "get_movie_quality_profiles", "get_movie_root_folders", "get_movie_download_clients",
   451→		"get_movie_blocklist", "manual_movie_search", "update_movie_monitoring", "delete_movie",
   452→		"trigger_movie_search", "grab_movie_release", "remove_movie_blocklist_item":
   453→		if a.radarr == nil {
   454→			return jsonError("Radarr integration is not configured"), true
   455→		}
   456→		switch name {
   457→		case "search_movies":
   458→			var input searchMoviesInput
   459→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   460→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   461→			}
   462→			result, err = a.radarr.Search(ctx, input.Term)
   463→		case "add_movie":
   464→			var input addMovieInput
   465→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {

   467→			}
   468→			minAvail := input.MinimumAvailability
   469→			if minAvail == "" {
   470→				minAvail = "released"
   471→			}
   472→			result, err = a.radarr.Add(ctx, radarr.AddMovieRequest{
   473→				Title:               input.Title,
   474→				TMDBID:              input.TMDBID,
   475→				QualityProfileID:    input.QualityProfileID,
   476→				RootFolderPath:      input.RootFolderPath,
   477→				Monitored:           true,
   478→				MinimumAvailability: minAvail,
   479→			})
   480→		case "get_movie_queue":
   481→			result, err = a.radarr.Queue(ctx)
   482→		case "get_movie_history":
   483→			var input getMovieHistoryInput
   484→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   485→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   486→			}
   487→			pageSize := input.PageSize
   488→			if pageSize == 0 {
   489→				pageSize = 20
   490→			}
   491→			result, err = a.radarr.History(ctx, pageSize)
   492→		case "check_movie_health":
   493→			result, err = a.radarr.Health(ctx)
   494→		case "remove_failed_movie":
   495→			var input removeFailedMovieInput
   496→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   497→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   498→			}
   499→			err = a.radarr.RemoveFailed(ctx, input.ID, input.Blocklist)
   500→			if err == nil {

   502→			}
   503→		case "get_movie_detail":
   504→			var input getMovieDetailInput
   505→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   506→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   507→			}
   508→			result, err = a.radarr.GetMovie(ctx, input.MovieID)
   509→		case "get_movie_quality_profiles":
   510→			result, err = a.radarr.GetQualityProfiles(ctx)
   511→		case "get_movie_root_folders":
   512→			result, err = a.radarr.GetRootFolders(ctx)
   513→		case "get_movie_download_clients":
   514→			result, err = a.radarr.GetDownloadClients(ctx)
   515→		case "get_movie_blocklist":
   516→			var input getMovieBlocklistInput
   517→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   518→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   519→			}
   520→			pageSize := input.PageSize
   521→			if pageSize == 0 {
   522→				pageSize = 20
   523→			}
   524→			result, err = a.radarr.GetBlocklist(ctx, pageSize)
   525→		case "manual_movie_search":
   526→			var input manualMovieSearchInput
   527→			if err := json.Unmarshal(rawInput, &input); err != nil {
   528→				return jsonError("invalid input: " + err.Error()), true
   529→			}
   530→			result, err = a.radarr.ManualSearch(ctx, input.MovieID)
   531→		case "update_movie_monitoring":

   105→// requestIDKey is the context key for the per-request identifier used in audit logs.

   120→// buildSystemPrompt returns the system prompt with pinned notebook notes appended.
   121→func (a *Agent) buildSystemPrompt(ctx context.Context) string {
   122→	dateLine := fmt.Sprintf("Today's date is %s.", time.Now().Format("2006-01-02"))
   123→
   124→	var locationLine string
   125→	if a.weather != nil {

   130→		}

   136→	} else {
   137→		prompt = fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n%s", dateLine, systemPrompt)
   138→	}

   151→	}

   155→	sb.WriteString("\n\n## Notebook (always loaded)\n")
   156→	for _, n := range pinned {

   160→		sb.WriteString(n.Content)

   166→// Process runs the agentic tool-use loop for a user message and returns the final text response.
   167→// History turns are prepended to provide conversational context.
   168→func (a *Agent) Process(ctx context.Context, userMessage string, history []Turn) (string, error) {
   169→	tools := toolDefinitions()
   170→
   171→	var messages []anthropic.MessageParam
   172→	for _, t := range history {
   173→		switch t.Role {
   174→		case "user":
   175→			messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(t.Content)))
   176→		case "assistant":
   177→			messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewAssistantMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(t.Content)))
   178→		}
   179→	}
   180→	messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(anthropic.NewTextBlock(userMessage)))
   181→
   182→	if a.limiter != nil {
   183→		a.limiter.Reset()
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	reqID := requestID(ctx)
   187→	sysPrompt := a.buildSystemPrompt(ctx)
   188→
   189→	for round := range maxToolRounds {
   190→		resp, err := a.client.Messages.New(ctx, anthropic.MessageNewParams{
   191→			Model:     anthropic.Model(a.model),
   192→			MaxTokens: a.maxTok,
   193→			System: []anthropic.TextBlockParam{
   194→				{Text: sysPrompt},
   195→			},
   196→			Messages: messages,
   197→			Tools:    tools,
   198→		})
   199→		if err != nil {
   200→			return "", fmt.Errorf("claude API call: %w", err)
   201→		}
   202→
   203→		// Collect tool uses from the response
   204→		var toolResults []anthropic.ContentBlockParamUnion
   205→		var textResponse string
   206→
   207→		for _, block := range resp.Content {
   208→			switch v := block.AsAny().(type) {
   209→			case anthropic.TextBlock:
   210→				textResponse += v.Text
   211→			case anthropic.ToolUseBlock:
   212→				result, isErr := a.executeToolWithAudit(ctx, v.Name, v.Input, round, reqID)
   213→				toolResults = append(toolResults, anthropic.NewToolResultBlock(v.ID, result, isErr))
   214→			}
   215→		}
   216→
   217→		// If no tool calls, we're done
   218→		if len(toolResults) == 0 {
   219→			return textResponse, nil
   220→		}
   221→
   222→		// Add assistant response and tool results to conversation
   223→		messages = append(messages, resp.ToParam())
   224→		messages = append(messages, anthropic.NewUserMessage(toolResults...))
   225→	}
   226→
   227→	return "", fmt.Errorf("exceeded maximum tool rounds (%d)", maxToolRounds)
   228→}

   230→// executeToolWithAudit wraps tool dispatch with rate limiting and audit logging.
   231→func (a *Agent) executeToolWithAudit(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage, round int, reqID string) (string, bool) {
   232→	// Audit log: invocation
   233→	a.logger.Info("tool invocation",
   234→		"tool", name,
   235→		"input", sanitizeInput(rawInput),
   236→		"round", round,
   237→		"request_id", reqID,
   238→	)
   239→
   240→	// Rate limit check
   241→	if a.limiter != nil {
   242→		if err := a.limiter.Allow(name, IsDestructive(name)); err != nil {
   243→			a.logger.Warn("tool rate limited",
   244→				"tool", name,
   245→				"error", err,
   246→				"round", round,
   247→				"request_id", reqID,
   248→			)
   249→			return jsonError(err.Error()), true
   250→		}
   251→	}
   252→
   253→	start := time.Now()
   254→	result, isErr := a.dispatchTool(ctx, name, rawInput)
   255→	duration := time.Since(start)
   256→
   257→	// Audit log: result
   258→	a.logger.Info("tool result",
   259→		"tool", name,
   260→		"success", !isErr,
   261→		"duration_ms", duration.Milliseconds(),
   262→		"round", round,
   263→		"request_id", reqID,
   264→	)
   265→
   266→	return result, isErr
   267→}

   269→// sanitizeInput returns a string summary of tool input suitable for logging.
   270→// It strips any field whose key contains sensitive substrings to avoid leaking credentials.
   271→func sanitizeInput(raw json.RawMessage) string {
   272→	var m map[string]any
   273→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
   274→		return "<invalid json>"
   275→	}
   276→	for k := range m {
   277→		lowerK := strings.ToLower(k)
   278→		if strings.Contains(lowerK, "key") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "secret") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "password") || strings.Contains(lowerK, "token") {
   279→			m[k] = "REDACTED"
   280→		}
   281→	}
   282→	out, err := json.Marshal(m)
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		return "<failed to sanitize json>"
   285→	}
   286→	return string(out)
   287→}
   288→
   289→// dispatchTool executes a tool call and returns the JSON result string and whether it's an error.
   290→func (a *Agent) dispatchTool(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage) (string, bool) {
   291→	if result, handled := a.dispatchSonarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
   292→		return result, true
   293→	}
   294→	if result, handled := a.dispatchRadarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
   295→		return result, true
   296→	}
   297→	if result, handled := a.dispatchProwlarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
   298→		return result, true
   299→	}
   300→	if result, handled := a.dispatchOverseerr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
   301→		return result, true
   302→	}
   303→	if result, handled := a.dispatchNotebook(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
   304→		return result, true
   305→	}
   306→	return jsonError("unknown tool: " + name), true
   307→}

   310→// On failure, it returns a non-empty JSON error string.
   311→func (a *Agent) findIndexer(ctx context.Context, id int) (*prowlarr.Indexer, string) {
   312→	indexers, err := a.prowlarr.ListIndexers(ctx)
   313→	if err != nil {
   314→		return nil, jsonError(err.Error())
   315→	}
   316→	for i := range indexers {

   318→			return &indexers[i], ""
   319→		}
   320→	}
   321→	return nil, jsonError(fmt.Sprintf("indexer %d not found", id))
   322→}

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/39

The refactor splits the two monolithic files into 12 files total:
- **tools.go** — shared infrastructure only (toolDef, generateSchema, allToolDefs, destructiveTools, IsDestructive)
- **tools_{sonarr,radarr,prowlarr,overseerr,notebook}.go** — per-service tool definitions + input structs
- **dispatch_{sonarr,radarr,prowlarr,overseerr,notebook}.go** — per-service dispatch methods returning `(result, isErr, handled)`
- **agent.go** — `dispatchTool()` now delegates to per-service dispatchers in sequence

All existing tests pass without modification.
